payload.summary: This week's review pulse highlights concerns around user experience, technical issues, financial and transactional concerns, and support and security. Users have reported issues with the app's UI, UPI transactions, and withdrawals. Some have also experienced frustration with customer support and delays in resolving issues. However, some users have praised the app's ease of use, investment options, and prompt customer support.
quotes: The new update has a very bad UI and UX. Portfolio is not selected by default.
I love the app! Before, stopping an entry took several seconds before it actually stopped, and those extra seconds were always added too, making everything frustrating to deal with.
Every trading app must be freezed to protect general public because it's not market it's an evil thing that caught peoples to theft their money
action_items: Improve the app's UI and UX to enhance user experience and resolve reported issues
Enhance customer support by providing prompt and effective resolutions to user concerns
Address technical issues, such as UPI transaction errors and withdrawal complications, to improve overall user satisfaction
action_ideas: Improve the app's UI and UX to enhance user experience and resolve reported issues
Enhance customer support by providing prompt and effective resolutions to user concerns
Address technical issues, such as UPI transaction errors and withdrawal complications, to improve overall user satisfaction

explanation_bullets: A mutual fund exit load is a fee charged to investors when they redeem their units from a mutual fund scheme.
The exit load is typically a percentage of the redemption amount and is deducted from the investor's proceeds.
The purpose of an exit load is to discourage investors from withdrawing their money from a mutual fund scheme too quickly.
The exit load fee is usually waived if the investor holds the units for a specified period, known as the lock-in period.
The exit load percentage and lock-in period vary from one mutual fund scheme to another.
The exit load is disclosed in the mutual fund scheme's offer document and website.
A brokerage fee is a charge levied by a broker or brokerage firm for facilitating transactions, such as buying or selling securities.
The fee is typically a percentage of the transaction amount or a flat rate per transaction.
Brokerage fees can vary depending on the type of investment, the broker or firm, and the services provided.
These fees are usually deducted from the investor's account at the time of the transaction.
Brokerage fees can be charged for a range of services, including trading, investment advice, and account maintenance.
The fees are a source of revenue for brokers and brokerage firms, allowing them to operate and provide services to investors.

INDmoney offers useful features, but constant UI/UX changes make it frustrating to use. Frequent updates shift menus and navigation, forcing users to relearn the app again and again. Instead of improving usability, many changes create confusion and slow down simple tasks. A finance app should feel stable and predictable. Please focus on consistency and meaningful improvements rather than frequent redesigns.
